summary: The test suite covers core requirements (parsing, license classification, reporting, and end-to-end workflow execution) with three well-structured tiers: unit tests (checker.test.ts), workflow structure tests, and integration tests via act. However, gaps limit confidence in error handling: parseRequirementsTxt has a throw for malformed lines that goes untested in unit tests (only caught via act); CLI file I/O errors (missing manifest, missing/invalid config) are not unit-tested; edge cases like empty manifests and concurrent scenarios are missing. Unit tests thoroughly cover happy paths and some edge cases (case-insensitivity, version cleaning, deny priority). The act test harness is well-designed and properly cleans up, but is limited to 3 test cases by the task constraint—though those cases cover the main workflows. Workflow structure tests verify YAML syntax and actionlint validation but don't assert behavior beyond file existence. The suite would catch regressions in the happy path but would miss bugs in error scenarios and untested branches.
